title: Kobresia filifolia
taxonomy: Kobresia filifolia is classified as a species of sedge, belonging to the family Cyperaceae. This family includes a wide range of sedges and rushes that are commonly found in wetlands and aquatic ecosystems. Kobresia filifolia is further classified within the genus Kobresia, which comprises several species of sedges found primarily in the Arctic and alpine regions. The species is often characterized by its delicate, thread-like leaves and its ability to thrive in areas with poor soil quality. Kobresia filifolia is native to the mountainous regions of Asia and is often found growing in moist, grassy areas.
